question: How do you know about Thermal sensor connector (2-pin T_SENSOR) of a motherboard?
content:
The 2-pin T_SENSOR connector is for a thermistor cable. Connect the separately purchased thermistor cable to T_SENSOR1; the connector pins are GND and SENSOR IN.<PIC>
It lets the motherboard monitor the temperature of critical motherboard components and connected devices. In BIOS, the Monitor menu can display T_Sensor Temperature, and T_Sensor can be selected as the temperature source for Chassis Fan 1/2/3 or Extension Fan 1/2/3 Q-Fan Source when those Q-Fan controls are enabled.
ret: "The 2-pin T_SENSOR connector is for a thermistor cable. Connect the separately purchased thermistor cable to T_SENSOR1; the connector pins are GND and SENSOR IN.<PIC>\nIt lets the motherboard monitor the temperature of critical motherboard components and connected devices. In BIOS, the Monitor menu can display T_Sensor Temperature, and T_Sensor can be selected as the temperature source for Chassis Fan 1/2/3 or Extension Fan 1/2/3 Q-Fan Source when those Q-Fan controls are enabled.",["Manual25_43"]
notes: Answered from the Motherboard manual section for the 2-pin T_SENSOR connector, with one figure used for the connector location, label, pin names, and separately purchased thermistor cable note.
